meaning: n.(多面体的)面,(宝石等的)刻面,小平面,方面,琢面vt.在...上刻画
examples: Now let's look at another facet of the problem.
The information revolution will touch every facet of composition, publication, distribution, and reading.
The idea that literature is an important facet of leadership development is not new.
Another important facet of the story is the potential demand for such a service.
One hundred years ago, most folks accepted adversity as an unavoidable facet of life.
